entry.title: The Sydney Morning Herald: 24 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: The Sydney Morning Herald: 24 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-24 07:06AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Prince Charles should keep an open mind about Rwanda deportation plan: Johnson
  • Albanese should send Putin a message by visiting Kyiv
  • How do you find your mojo?
  • Grab your brightest doof coat. Club clothing is back
  • Kathleen Folbigg chance to clear her name
  • The Wrap: ASX finishes positive week with tech rally
  • Former premier, health groups urge Perrottet to implement ice inquiry recommendations
  • Census time - here's what to expect
  • The latest illustrations from artist Cathy Wilcox
  • Best of cartoons, June 24, 2022
  • Electricity grid chaos to charge up power bills
  • The latest illustrations from artist Matt Davidson
  • Triple trepidation as weather forecasters warn of La Nina’s return
  • Self-help guru shares psychological test that reveals your deepest desires and fears
  • Millions to pass through airports today
  • Four senior Victorian ministers to leave the Andrews government
  • Cost of CCC legal case against former Queensland deputy premier revealed
  • Sydney protestors arrested
  • Queensland scraps last of COVID-19 restrictions
  • Ministers hold urgent talks with union to head off Sydney train disruptions
  • Why Albanese is beefing up the bureaucracy
  • China envoy offers hope for better relations with Australia
  • Make or brake: The critical test for Albanese’s manufacturing vision
  • Greens threaten to amend budget unless government dumps top-end tax cuts
  • Investors warn funds for coal, gas imperil landmark energy reform
  • The latest illustrations from artist Simon Letch
  • The latest illustrations from artist Matt Golding
  • Best of cartoons, June 23, 2022
  • Brakes on spending: Albanese warns of budget cuts
  • Warning signs for air force as ADF comes under scrutiny over suicide risk
  • Network 10 hits out at ‘inaccurate, unfair’ reports on Wilkinson as new Lehrmann trial date set
  • Ukrainian ambassador urges Albanese to visit Zelensky, says it would ‘demonstrate solidarity’
  • Jets players discussed Dawson allegedly wanting to ‘get rid of’ wife
  • Police arrest two in second Blockade Australia raid
  • The gas fight in Narrabri
  • CCTV released as police investigate Nowra stabbing death
  • Unvaccinated teachers allowed back to work from next term under new proposal
  • Footage released as Nowra stabbing investigated
  • Mia used to barely attend school. Now she has perfect attendance
  • Free shots fail to boost flu vaccination rates, as COVID hospitalisations rise
  • Don’t get comfortable with COVID: pandemic leadership has left us exposed
  • From the Archives, 1992 : Bitter Greiner forced to quit
  • In the Herald : June 24, 1926
  • Energy altruism doesn’t extend to the Wi-Fi
  • Buying cocaine ‘as popular as buying food or alcohol’, judge says
  • EU grants Ukraine candidate status in ‘historic moment’
  • Xi’s dilemma: The unvaccinated elderly keeping COVID-zero China in lockdowns
  • 15 Republicans back Democrat gun bill in US Senate, breaking decades-long impasse
  • Gallery: The best photos from around the world
  • Trump investigation heats up
  • On the frontline of the war in Ukraine
  • Gun rights expanded after US Supreme Court overturns NY handgun law
  • Moment 'Good Samaritans' rush to help shark attack victim
  • With Sri Lanka in freefall, this is what a collapsed economy looks like
  • Trump attempted to corrupt his own Justice Dept, Jan 6 committee finds
  • ‘Magnificent’: World’s biggest bacterium found in Caribbean
  • Woman suffers burns to 60 per cent of body after being found on fire
  • Juul e-cigarettes banned in the US after FDA ruling
  • Lockdowns spark signs of defiance among China’s restless youth
  • More than 1000 people die in Afghanistan earthquake
  • Eight Maradona doctors, nurses to be tried for homicide
  • Australia news LIVE: Albanese warns of cuts to federal budget; Ukraine given candidate status by European Union; ABS under fire over ‘non-binary’ census question
  • Failed mayoral candidate claimed his COVID message cost him election
  • ‘It was messy at home’: A 50-year friendship forged from chaos
  • How Carl Lewis and Whitney Houston helped me accept myself
  • Letting go of a four-legged friend
  • Two minutes with Danny Katz: The calculated risks of allocated cinema seating
  • Why dancers make great lovers: Bangarra’s Stephen Page
  • Noise, chaos, subway rats, the smell of hot garbage: happily, New York hasn’t changed
  • Lost in space: Why city slickers are losing their spatial skills
  • Rapist’s late apology to victim no sign of remorse, prosecutors say
  • Cyclist's dangerous move on Sydney Harbour Bridge
  • No apology as Qantas promises to fix customer service
  • The industries where ‘green jobs’ are in demand
  • ‘Ups, downs, challenges’: Packer praises Crown as US giant Blackstone takes reins
  • Probuild creditors set to vote on compensation deal
  • ‘Golden run’ at risk: Reality bites after Amazon’s warehouse admission
  • These ingredients mean the $50 main course is here to stay
  • From casinos to houses: Why Australia remains a money laundering haven
  • The Fed could make a US recession even worse if it overreacts
  • This time it is different - the crypto crash is real
  • Qantas announces flight cuts, Jetstar CEO to depart
  • ‘Investment ready’: Billionaire-backed Sun Cable’s clean energy project gets another tick
  • Crypto’s frozen mystery: The fate of billions is up in the air
  • There’s blooming good careers in the garden
  • For the love of teaching, it’s a testing time
  • Who gets fired over social media posts? We studied hundreds of cases to find out
  • Regulators home in on banks’ skinny deposit rate rises
  • The office monsters are trying to claw their way back to 2019
  • Woolworths offers rapid deliveries for $5 via new Metro60 app
  • ‘Don’t fall in love with Rupert’: Murdoch, Hall head for divorce
  • The best movies, TV, music, podcasts and books of the year (so far)
  • Skip the boring bits, hit a chapter a week, get drunk: how to read Ulysses
  • Twenty million programs and still counting: that’s the BBC at 100
  • In this empty shop two artists opened a window to a dystopian future
  • 5 home furniture pieces that will last a lifetime
  • Shocking crimes and Minnie Driver’s eye-opening memoir: Top books to read next
  • Keir Choreographic Award: Engrossing but not a dance step in sight
  • The Undeclared War official trailer
  • Streaming is getting more expensive. So which service is right for you?
  • Who hated Robert Menzies enough to take a knife to his official portrait?
  • Logies 2022 In Memoriam: Remembering those we've lost
  • Hibiscus by The Huxleys
  • Ferocious, topical and sassy: the new show you cannot, must not miss
  • There’s much we can still learn from these 12 life-changing experiments
  • ‘Love at first sight’: How Martin, Short and Gomez became TV’s hottest property
  • The ‘bonkers’ self-published book making history as a Miles Franklin contender
  • National weather forecast for Friday June 24
  • How Lismore’s worst ever flood unfolded
Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: Korea Herald: 24 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • [Newsmaker] Approval of South Korea’s 1st homegrown vaccine likely next week
  • Lee Jun-seok at risk of penalty by ethics panel while his supporters grow vocal
  • NSC intervened in ministry decision on South Korean killed by NK: lawmaker
  • House panel advances 2023 defense bill maintaining US troop level in S. Korea
  • Yoon says revision of 52-hour work week not yet official
  • New COVID-19 cases below 8,000 for 2nd day amid easing omicron spread
  • N. Korea's new suspected COVID-19 cases under 20,000 for 6th day: state media
  • In key party meeting, N. Korea approves strengthening 'war deterrent,' state media says
  • Ukraine lawmakers to visit Seoul next month: ruling party
  • S. Korea to make work schedule more flexible, encourage performance-based pay model
  • North Korea’s COVID-19 crisis: How bad is it and what’s next after May wave
  • N. Korean military discusses changes to operational plan, frontline forces’ mission
  • Panel expert downbeat on Korean investments in premium TV displays
  • Porsche Korea CEO elected new KGCCI head
  • Posco apologizes for sexual violence in workplace
  • Construction contracts rise 10.7% in value in Q1
  • [Behind the Wheel] Lexus’ first plug-in hybrid offers smooth, economical yet luxury driving
  • Hyundai Mobis system able to check multiple vital signs in cars
  • Government to push retailers for gasoline price cut
  • S. Korea's fuel consumption jumps in May despite higher oil prices: data
  • Trade deficit expected to widen in June on fewer working days, strike: official
  • Seoul shares open higher after extended market rout
  • Won sinks to 13-year low, drags Kospi further down
  • Seoul stocks again gripped by panic selling on recession fears; Korean won at 13-year low
  • Memories of Korean War outbreak on June 25 transcended in art
  • [Eye interview] Hair stylist-turned-artist realizes his vision on a mountain side resort
  • [Well-curated weekend] Learn about mythical creatures of Korean legends to better understand Korean dramas
  • [Around the Hotels] Promotions and packages
  • [Travel Bits] Festivals, sights across Korea
  • ‘Taekwon, Fly Up!’ presents charm of taekwondo with music and dance
  • Two decades of dedication to young talent pays off at international classical music competitions
  • [Box Office] Movies in theaters this week
  • [Today’s K-pop] Seventeen confirms Japan tour plan
  • No longer nameless, Lee Mu-jin shows stories before he made it big through first EP
  • [Graphic News] S. Korea ranks No. 1 in household debt-to-GDP ratio in Q1
  • [Tyler Cowen] Beware the nested games of Russia’s war in Ukraine
  • [Editorial] Taking off

entry.title: Washington Post: 24 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Ukraine to withdraw troops from besieged Severodonetsk
  • Kyiv granted E.U. membership candidacy in symbolic win amid war
  • Russia targeted Ukrainian ammunition to weaken Kyiv on the battlefield
  • British Conservative Party chair quits after new election losses
  • Afghanistan endures yet another deadly shock
  • WHO weighs declaring monkeypox a global emergency as European cases surge
  • Sri Lanka’s economy has ‘completely collapsed,’ prime minister says
  • E.U. has backed candidate status for Ukraine. Here’s what that means.
  • E.U. leaders make Ukraine a candidate for membership
  • Traces of highly contagious poliovirus found in British sewage
  • U.S. monkeypox response mirrors early coronavirus missteps, experts say
  • Thousands of ‘witches’ could be posthumously pardoned in Scotland
  • U.S. woman denied abortion in Malta to be airlifted out amid fears for her life
  • Afghans mourn the dead, search for shelter after devastating earthquake
  • Amid criticism, Western tourists returning to a battered Syria
  • After restaurant attack, authorities continue to gaslight China’s women
  • Death toll from Afghanistan's quake rises to 1,150 people
  • India flies tons of essentials to quake-rocked Afghanistan
  • India sends team to help with deadly Afghanistan earthquake
  • 1 dead, dozens hurt in 12th day of protesting in Ecuador
  • Prosecutor: 4 police, up to 8 suspects killed in west Mexico
  • N. Korea approves new frontline army duties amid tensions
  • Hong Kongers reflect on Taiwan, an imperfect exile
  • Afghans bury dead, dig for survivors of devastating quake
  • Brazil: Raped girl, 11, gets abortion after initial refusal
  • Ohio health agency staffer fired for abortion pill mention in newsletter
  • 6 killed after ‘Huey’ helicopter crashes in West Virginia
  • Allstate, Progressive drop company over racist Juneteenth sign
  • Publix won’t offer coronavirus vaccines to children under 5 ‘at this time’
  • They protested with Pride flags at graduation. Their fight isn’t over.
  • Alexa has a new voice — your dead relative’s
  • A half-century after one movement, ‘Fierce Madres’ in Uvalde call for another
  • She had ‘soulmate’ kill her husband. A judge gave her life in prison.
  • He stole a patrol vehicle, officials say — then responded to an emergency
  • Echoes of Watergate: Trump’s appointees reveal his push to topple Justice Dept.
  • Rep. Scott Perry played key role in promoting false claims of fraud
  • Jan. 6 panel names five Republicans who allegedly sought Trump pardons
  • Home of Jeffrey Clark, Trump DOJ official, searched by federal agents
  • Jan. 6 hearing recap: Trump White House aides say multiple GOP members of Congress asked for pardons

  • As Biden touts gas tax pause, even some of his own officials balk
  • House panel approves expanding anti-money laundering reporting requirements
  • How a teen lifeguard took on the gender pay gap — and what she gained
  • NASA declares testing of SLS moon rocket complete. Next up: Launch.
  • Mortgage rates continue to escalate, move closer to 6 percent
  • Distinguished homes for sale in the D.C. region
  • How a ‘move manager’ can help you trim down on your belongings
  • What I love about my home in Portland, Ore.
  • Yes, You’ll Still Need Me When I’m 94
  • ‘90s House and Fashion Are Back — in Time for a 1970s Economy
  • Europe Must Declare a War Economy
  • Real Stress Hurts Bank Buybacks More Than Fed’s Test
  • A Basic Guide to ESG Investing and Why it Faces a Backlash: QuickTake
  • What You Need to Know About Recessions — Including Whether We’re in One
  • Here’s How US States Differ on Carrying Guns in Public
  • What’s a Reverse Currency War and Who’s Fighting One?
  • Trump and his crackpots brought us to the brink of ‘losing it all’
  • Russia’s oil earnings are booming. The U.S. and Europe need a new plan.
  • Biden needs to score in the culture wars
  • The Supreme Court’s gun ruling is a serious misfire
  • Democrats had an opportunity for a major political realignment. They blew it.
  • It will be bad if Merrick Garland prosecutes Trump — and worse if he doesn’t
  • John Hinckley paid for attacking democracy. Will Trump’s cronies?
  • Eric Greitens’s ‘RINO hunting’ ad shows the radicalization pipeline in the GOP
  • Why the gun deal won’t trigger a conservative revolt
  • The Washington Post guide to writing an opinion article
  • The Jan. 6 hearings’ slippery slope
  • 5 lessons from Thursday’s devastating Jan. 6 hearing
  • Happy the elephant had her day in court. We humans are better for it.
  • Lithuanian President Gitanas Nauseda: Now is the time to make NATO even stronger
  • No separation of Christianity and state for this court
  • Activist justice for all
  • Why France’s overseas territories are voting for anyone but Macron
  • Biden must not betray defenders of democracy in Saudi Arabia
  • Deflection doesn’t end racism
  • Children want financial literacy classes, too
  • A vital lesson of Juneteenth has largely been ignored
  • Families are using, not abusing, the child tax credit
  • My description of this accomplished pianist was not an afterthought
  • The New Union Station should embrace its history
  • There is a better option to keep Trump out of office than prosecution
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 24 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 24 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-24 07:00AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Hong Kong police arrest 1,800 people, aged 11 to 90, in first 4 weeks of cross-border crime crackdown
  • Hong Kong’s next leader John Lee speaks to the Post
  • Coronavirus: Beijing making contingency plans for Hong Kong government swearing-in ceremony on July 1 after 2 top city officials catch Covid-19
  • Hong Kong’s Jumbo Floating Restaurant has not ‘sunk’ only ‘capsized’, company insists, but spokeswoman can’t say if it’s still above water
  • Darkening skies (2012-2017): Hong Kong’s years of simmering tensions in 25 photos
  • UBS’s top China banker says Hong Kong still a magnet for global IPOs, with additional reforms likely to attract more start-ups in Greater Bay Area
  • Weighing up Hong Kong’s legal system 25 years after handover: making sure the law still rules
  • Hong Kong must not cling to governance structures of the past
  • Hong Kong lawmakers, business leaders call for cutting hotel quarantine to 3 days after incoming leader John Lee reveals reopening options
  • Government efforts to spur acceptance of ethnic minority groups in Hong Kong kindergartens prove effective, as intake in preschools grow
  • Hong Kong’s public hospitals unveil scheme to hire doctors from Greater Bay Area in bid to ease manpower crunch
  • Lawmakers and engineers call for safety review of power cable bridges after fire led to Hong Kong’s worst outage in decades
  • Opening of Hong Kong Palace Museum a milestone in the Jockey Club’s support for the HKSAR in the past 25 years
  • Hong Kong police officer coerced teen into taking blame for school fire two years ago, judge rules
  • Hong Kong police arrest unemployed man over indecent assault of 12-year-old girl at park toilet
  • Coronavirus: infection of 2 top Hong Kong officials raises concerns over state leader visit for 25th anniversary of handover
  • Obesity among Hong Kong children increased during coronavirus pandemic due to unhealthy lifestyle, survey shows
  • Second Chinese rights activist Ding Jiaxi stands trial on state subversion charges in secret proceeding
  • Video: China’s Chang’e 5 lunar lander finds water on the moon, but not as much as they hoped
  • Video: China to send rocket on collision course with asteroid to knock it into safer orbit
  • Business groups urge Biden to return trade to heart of Asia policies
  • LGBT rights in China: transgender youth seeking gender affirming surgery find family approval still biggest hurdle
  • China, South Korea battle population woes as ‘children are not a must’, adding to economic peril
  • China’s world’s factory tag threatened by Vietnam, but ‘there’s nothing to worry about’, analysts say
  • Why China should brace itself for a Pacific rebuff
  • Chinese radar breakthrough will see commercial drones soar in power and applications, says Shenzhen team
  • China’s aircraft carrier No 4 will not catch up with US Navy’s nuclear-powered giants, analysts say
  • China ‘commends’ Nepal’s decision to reject US partnership programme
  • US-China tech war: top Chinese scientist envisions forked RISC-V chip design standard to cushion decoupling impact
  • China floats BRICS free-trade deal, while Russia’s Vladimir Putin calls for an alternative to US dollar
  • China’s strict Covid-19 border controls show more signs of easing, as Zhejiang province cuts entry quarantine
  • China’s zero-Covid policy hammered Beijing’s economy in May. But how bad was it compared to Shanghai?
  • Chinese video gaming revenue falls in May, a third month of decline amid slowing economy and less player spending
  • ‘Mini hotels’ for sea creatures reveal Hong Kong’s rich marine life
  • China’s payments, fintech sectors to ‘play bigger role’ in boosting economy, President Xi says, in positive signal for Big Tech
  • China’s ambassador urges new Australian government to repair ties
  • Birthday giveaway: Richest Asian Gautam Adani pledges US$7.7 billion for social causes
  • North Korea approves ‘stronger war deterrent’ at key military meeting
  • Can Indonesia’s Jokowi breathe easy, with air pollution in Jakarta and other cities still causing tens of thousands of premature deaths?
  • Myanmar’s Suu Kyi moved to solitary confinement in prison, but said to be ‘in strong spirits’
  • South China Sea: Philippines, China abandon joint energy exploration talks
  • Malaysia: Were Mahathir’s remarks about ‘reclaiming’ Singapore and Indonesia’s Riau Islands aimed at Johor’s sultan?
  • Malaysia’s role pressuring Myanmar junta praised by UN human rights expert
  • Okinawa wants US troops out – 77 years after bloody battle
  • Philippines: Making a splash in the ‘mermaiding’ subculture that’s got fans hooked worldwide
  • Bring cannabis to Indonesia and you could face death penalty, Thai embassy tells tourists
  • Balkans complain about stalled EU bids as war-torn Ukraine gets candidate status
  • Covid-19 vaccines ‘saved 20 million lives’ in first year, study says
  • UK’s Boris Johnson suffers double by-election defeat humiliation
  • Pink Floyd seeking US$500 million for music catalogue
  • UAE sells US$3 billion in high-quality debt amid recession fears
  • US sending Ukraine US$450 million more in arms, including rocket systems, says White House
  • Bill Cosby to appeal civil ruling on teen sex assault
  • Nike to make full exit from Russia due to war in Ukraine
  • European Union agrees to make Ukraine a candidate for EU membership
  • US Senate passes gun safety bill as Supreme Court makes it easier for Americans to carry handguns
  • British Airways set for ‘summer of strikes’ at London’s Heathrow
  • US$156 million Russian superyacht in Dubai tests US-UAE ties amid sanctions
  • Instagram to protect young users with new AI tool to verify age
  • Ukraine: Russian soldier rape trial begins, first of many cases
  • EU leaders to set war-torn Ukraine on road to membership
  • Alibaba, XPeng drive Hong Kong stocks to two-week high on China recovery outlook, Xi pledge
  • GogoX shares rise in HKEX trading debut, as the listing of Hong Kong’s first unicorn marks the return of the ceremonial gong after two-year silence
  • Two dead after Nio car falls from third floor of headquarters building in Shanghai during test drive
  • HKEX’s London Metal Exchange names Oliver Wyman to review nickel trading chaos amid investor lawsuits
  • Look to portfolio resilience in times of volatility
  • What the bitcoin bloodbath reveals about the cryptocurrency market
  • Hong Kong audit regulator in talks with Beijing’s Ministry of Finance about granting it access to working papers of mainland Chinese companies, chairman says
  • In boost for Hong Kong buyers, UK home prices have trailed those in EU states in six years since Brexit vote
  • China considers extending EV tax exemption to put industry back on track after Covid-19 lockdown
  • China bans over 30 live-streaming behaviours, demands qualifications to discuss law, finance, medicine
  • Hong Kong, 25 years after its return to China, needs a new narrative, not nostalgia
  • Talent flourishes in Hong Kong’s vibrant I&T ecosystem, with global and diverse minds developing careers in the city
  • Apple and Android phones hacked by Italian spyware, Google says
  • CityU students’ blockchain-powered solution for used car buyer protection wins bronze in the Fintech Olympiad 2022
  • Best new Hong Kong restaurants: Italian, French Japanese, fusion – fresh concepts from the King of Truffles, the Demon Chef, and more
  • How Hong Kong developed a world-leading port from the ashes of war, and factories, and its maritime future
  • Indonesia, Portugal and South Africa mull digital-nomad visas, but how to define exactly what ‘work’ is and where it is happening?
  • Welcomed with leis and ukuleles on an extraordinary South Pacific cruise by cargo ship from Tahiti, tourists revel in Raivavae, Rapa and the other Austral Islands of French Polynesia
  • VELA CASA showcases charismatic, art-level European designer brand furniture
  • Netflix K-drama review: Money Heist: Korea – Joint Economic Area Part 1 gives a slick geopolitical makeover to hit Spanish thriller series
  • Japan embraces CBD sold in drinks, candies and vapes at cafes and health stores, but keeps strict laws on marijuana’s recreational use
  • Hong Kong Fringe Club mounts what could be its farewell show, a retrospective of art exhibitions from the past four decades
  • K-drama review: Woori the Virgin – bubbly romcom starring Lim Soo-hyang recovers for a romantic photo finish
  • Hong Kong-style milk tea brand Hoko in London is giving emigrants a welcome taste of home
  • Do prohibitive ‘foreigner fees’ really keep expats out? Singapore slapped a 35 per cent stamp duty on overseas property investors – Bali, Canada, Thailand and New Zealand all blocked international buyers
  • Inside Matthew Williams’ first Givenchy menswear show at Paris Men’s Fashion Week: his spring/summer 2023 collection channelled NYC street style – but was it high fashion enough for the runway?
  • 7 K-pop stars openly supporting the LGBT community, from Red Velvet’s Yeri, Girls’ Generation’s Tiffany, 2NE1’s CL and Le Sserafim’s Yunjin – who posted a throwback pic to celebrate Pride Month
  • Who is Titus Low, Singapore’s controversial influencer? His OnlyFans page landed him in hot water, but he now drives an AMG Mercedes, launched his own line of NFTs and might’ve married Cheryl Chin
  • Meet Samuel Chatto, Queen Elizabeth’s heartthrob grandnephew: the British royal is 29th in line to the throne, a pottery artist and qualified yoga instructor who trained in India
  • Meet Jude Law’s mirror-image son Rafferty – from modelling for Dolce & Gabbana and campaigning for PETA, to acting in Masters of the Air with Austin Butler, he could become just as famous as his dad
  • Are Austin Butler and Kaia Gerber showbiz’s hottest couple? The Elvis star and supermodel have flaunted their relationship on red carpets from the Met Gala to Cannes, and mum Cindy Crawford approves
  • Meet Elon Musk’s first wife Justine Wilson – the fantasy author was married to the billionaire Tesla CEO for 8 years, and is supporting their twin child Vivian, who just came out as a trans woman
  • Milan Men’s Fashion Week: Zegna flaunted light, minimalist pieces connected to the earth – tying in with the luxury brand’s sustainable materials and practices
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Financial Times: 24 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

  • Chinese memory chip king’s rapid growth, Musk’s China challenges

  • Fees increasingly on the agenda for wealth management clients
  • Government bonds rally on fears of global economic slowdown
  • UK oil and gas producers warn Sunak over windfall tax
  • Index rejigs: demotion would teach Meta the value of humility
  • Copper price at 16-month low as slowdown fears hit industrial metals
  • Crypto enthusiasts are betting the house on creative destruction
  • Why it’s getting harder to kick our fossil fuel addiction
  • My hot take on the future of heatwaves

entry.title: NY Times: 24 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Supreme Court Strikes Down New York Law Limiting Guns in Public
  • In One Day, Washington Goes in Two Directions on Guns
  • Here’s What Is In the Senate’s Gun Bill — And What Was Left Out

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
